key_insight: The paper shows that an LLM can help construct a fully verified proof in logic programming, but only when its output is checked by a formal theorem prover (LPTP).
engineering_relevance: It demonstrates a practical human-in-the-loop workflow for using AI to assist theorem proving while preserving formal correctness.
method: Start from basic pure logic programming predicates.
Sketch the classic irrationality proof of √2 in LPTP using natural deduction.
Use an LLM to help generate proof steps, then fully verify the result with LPTP.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a case study that ends with a complete formal proof, partially generated by an LLM and fully proof-checked by LPTP.
watch_item: This is a single case study on one classic theorem, so the approach’s reliability and scalability beyond a simple proof remain uncertain.
paper_title: Case study: proving sqrt(2) irrational with LPTP and an LLM
